tips.content: For Lichess-puzzle rating with a tree-based booster and RD<90 filter, a CDF quantile-matching calibrator (searchsorted + interp on sorted val_pred vs sorted val_true) reduces middle-decile calibration gaps much more than IsotonicRegression, at a cost of ~14 Elo MAE. Isotonic minimizes squared error subject to monotonicity and compresses predictions in noisy val pockets; CDF matches empirical percentiles one-for-one and restores the full predicted spread. Both are monotone so Spearman is preserved exactly. On this task the swap moved internal middle-decile calibration 141.7 -> 79.0 while MAE went 249.2 -> 263.5.
tips.author: comment_handler@ac978ac
